/** * PrototypeSelectorMock stories — visual mock of the prototype picker * submenu for CanvasCreateMenu. * * Scene 1: The panel in its default state (all prototypes listed) * Scene 2: Filtering in action * Scene 3: A prototype expanded to show its flows */ import { useState } from 'react' import { PrototypeSelectorPanel, ChevronRight } from './PrototypeSelectorMock.jsx' import styles from './PrototypeSelectorMock.module.css' /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 1 — Default state: all prototypes listed ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function DefaultState() { const [selected, setSelected] = useState(null) return (
Scene 1 — Prototype selector (default)

When the user clicks Prototype in the "Add to canvas" menu, a submenu opens with a searchable list of all prototypes. Prototypes with a single flow are directly selectable. Prototypes with multiple flows show a chevron — clicking expands the flow sub-items.

{selected && (
Selected
{selected}
)}
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 2 — With search filter active ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function FilteredState() { return (
Scene 2 — Filtering prototypes

The search input filters prototypes by name. Matching prototypes auto-expand to reveal their flows. This reuses the same{' '} SearchableList pattern from the Component selector.

When filtering, all matched prototypes show their flows expanded so the user can see exactly which flows matched their query.

{}} />
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 3 — Comparison: before/after ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function BeforeAfter() { return (
Scene 3 — Before → After

Before: clicking "Prototype" in the add menu creates an empty prototype widget that the user must then configure manually.
After: clicking "Prototype" opens a submenu where the user picks a specific prototype and flow — the widget is created pre-configured.

{}} />
) }

/** * Terminal Session Management — visual storyboard of the TMUX session * management experience: new terminal prompt, session picker, conflict * resolution, orphan recovery, and the full lifecycle flow. * * Future development (not in scope for Phase 2/3): * - Warm-start banner / session context files (.session.md) * - "Continue with Copilot" from committed context * - Cross-user session handover */ import styles from './TerminalSessionMock.module.css' import { Chrome, Line, Btn } from './TerminalSessionMock' /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 0 — New Terminal Prompt ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function NewTerminalPrompt() { return (
Scene 0 — New Terminal Widget

When a new terminal widget is added to the canvas (or an existing one has no session), the user sees a prompt to choose how to start. This replaces the blank shell.

} >
How would you like to start?
Sessions are scoped to this branch and canvas.
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 1 — Session Picker (This Canvas) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function SessionPickerThisCanvas() { return (
Scene 1 — Session Picker (Default: This Canvas)

User clicks the session button in the terminal toolbar (or hits a keyboard shortcut). Default view shows only sessions from the current canvas, scoped to the current branch.

} >
Select a session:
Sessions: This canvas {' '}All canvases{' '}All branches{' '} (tab to cycle)
{' # Status Modified Created Summary'}
── terminal-widget-plan-v6 ──
❯ 1. Live 2m ago 30m ago Copilot: migrate Button to CSS Modules ! Live
2. Background 15m ago 1h ago Shell: npm run build → tests
3. Archived 1h ago 3h ago Shell: debugging canvas perf
↑↓ navigate Enter select Esc cancel / search t open tmux (advanced)
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 2 — Session Picker (All Canvases) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function SessionPickerAllCanvases() { return (
Scene 2 — Session Picker (All Canvases, This Branch)

User tabs to "All canvases" — shows every session on the current branch, grouped by canvas name. Still no cross-branch sessions visible.

} >
Select a session:
Sessions: This canvas{' '} All canvases {' '}All branches{' '} (tab to cycle)
{' # Status Modified Created Summary'}
── terminal-widget-plan-v6 ──
❯ 1. Live 2m ago 30m ago Copilot: migrate Button to CSS Modules ! Live
2. Background 15m ago 1h ago Shell: npm run build → tests
── design-system ──
3. Background 20m ago 2h ago Copilot: refactor Dialog component
4. Archived 1h ago 3h ago Shell: debugging canvas perf
── button-patterns ──
5. Background 45m ago 4h ago Shell: npm run lint → fixes
↑↓ navigate Enter select Esc cancel / search t open tmux (advanced)
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 3 — Session Picker (All Branches) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function SessionPickerAllBranches() { return (
Scene 3 — Session Picker (All Branches)

User tabs to "All branches" — shows sessions across all worktrees, grouped by branch. Current branch appears first.

} >
Select a session:
Sessions: This canvas{' '}All canvases{' '} All branches {' '} (tab to cycle)
{' # Status Modified Created Summary'}
── 4.2.0 (current) ──
❯ 1. Live 2m ago 30m ago plan-v6 › Copilot: migrate Button ! Live
2. Background 20m ago 2h ago design-system › Copilot: refactor Dialog
── 4.1.0 ──
3. Archived 2h ago 1d ago design-system › Copilot: fix viewfinder routing ! Active processes
4. Archived 3h ago 2d ago test › Shell: npm audit
── main ──
5. Archived 1d ago 3d ago examples › Shell: setup demo data
↑↓ navigate Enter select Esc cancel / search t open tmux (advanced)
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 4 — Cross-Worktree Conflict ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function ConflictDialog() { return (
Scene 4 — Cross-Worktree Conflict

User opens a canvas on branch 4.2.0--tmux-management that has a terminal widget whose TMUX session is currently live on branch 4.2.0. The widget detects the conflict and presents a choice.

} >
⚠ Session conflict
This terminal's session is currently Live on another worktree:

{' '}Branch: 4.2.0
{' '}Canvas: design-system
{' '}Running: copilot (Copilot CLI — active conversation)

Detaching will disconnect it from the other worktree. The session content will be preserved.
Deleting the widget will not stop the session — it will be archived and recoverable from the session picker.
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 5 — Warm-Start Banner ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function WarmStartBanner() { return (
Scene 5 — Warm-Start (Session Context) · FUTURE DEVELOPMENT

Not in scope for initial implementation. Future feature: when a colleague opens a canvas with a terminal widget and no local session exists, a committed .session.md context file provides a warm-start banner to help them pick up where the previous author left off.

} >
Previous session by @dfosco
Last commit: a1b2c3d — "refactor: migrate Button to CSS modules"
Branch: 4.2.0 · Canvas: design-system · 12 widgets
{`## What Was Implemented - Button component fully migrated to CSS Modules - Button.story.jsx updated and passing ## What Was Interrupted - Dialog migration incomplete — 3 of 7 style blocks converted - Header migration not started ## Shell History (last 5) npm run build copilot "refactor Button component to use CSS modules" git diff --stat npm test -- --run src/components/Button/Button.test.js copilot "now migrate Dialog component"`}
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 6 — Orphan Recovery ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function OrphanRecovery() { return (
Scene 6 — Widget Deleted → Archived → Undo Recovery

User accidentally deletes a terminal widget that had an active Copilot session. The session is archived (not killed). A toast prompts ⌘Z to undo — if they do, the widget is restored and reconnects automatically. Otherwise the session remains archived and can be found later in the sessions list.

{/* Step 1: Before delete */}
Step 1: Widget active with Copilot session
}>
↓ user accidentally deletes widget
{/* Step 2: Archived notification */}
Step 2: Session archived, not killed
⚠ Widget removed. Session archived for 5 minutes.
Hit ⌘Z to undo and reconnect.
↓ user hits ⌘Z (undo)
{/* Step 3: Reconnected */}
Step 3: Widget restored, session reconnected seamlessly
}>
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 7 — Full Lifecycle Flow ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function FullLifecycle() { return (
Scene 7 — Full Session Lifecycle

The complete journey: create widget → session scoped to branch/canvas → switch sessions → cross-worktree conflict → archive on delete → context file committed → colleague picks up work.

{/* Left column */}
1. Create
Add terminal widget to canvas
→ TMUX session: sb-4.2.0--plan-v6--terminal-abc
→ Registry: status = Live
2. Work
Run commands, launch Copilot
→ Session summary auto-updates
→ Context captured periodically
3. Switch
Open session picker → select #3
→ Old session: Background
→ New session: Live
{/* Right column */}
4. Delete Widget
Widget removed from canvas
→ Session NOT killed
→ Registry: status = Archived
→ Grace timer: 5 min
5. Context Saved · FUTURE
On archive, capture:
→ Last commit + canvas state
→ Planned / implemented / interrupted
→ Write to assets/terminal-sessions/
→ Committed to git
6. Colleague Picks Up · FUTURE
Opens canvas → terminal widget
→ No local session found
→ Reads .session.md
→ Warm-start: "Continue with Copilot"
) }

/** * Terminal Widget Mockup stories — a visual storyboard of the * terminal-on-canvas experience, from idle shell to Copilot integration. */ import styles from './TerminalWidgetMock.module.css' /* ── Shared primitives (inline for story self-containment) ── */ function Chrome({ title, status, actions, children }) { return (
⬛ {title} {status && {status}} {actions &&
{actions}
}
{children}
) } function Line({ prompt, command, output, muted }) { return (
{prompt && {prompt}} {command && {command}} {output &&
{output}
}
) } function Btn({ icon, label, active }) { return ( ) } function CopilotBadge() { return ✦ Copilot } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 1 — Empty terminal widget on canvas ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function EmptyTerminal() { return (
Scene 1 — Idle Terminal

A terminal widget on the canvas, connected to a local shell via WebSocket + node-pty. Behaves like any other canvas widget — drag, resize, toolbar actions.

} >
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 2 — Running a command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function RunningCommand() { return (
Scene 2 — Shell in use

Full interactive shell — run builds, tests, git commands. Keyboard input goes to the terminal when focused; Escape returns focus to canvas.

} >
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 3 — Copilot launcher ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function CopilotLauncher() { return (
Scene 3 — Launch Copilot

The toolbar has a "✦ Copilot" button that sends copilot to the PTY. Canvas context (selected widgets, sticky notes) is auto-attached as initial context.

} > `} />
) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 4 — Connected widgets as context ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function CanvasContext() { return (
Scene 4 — Widget Connectors

Widgets are connected to the terminal via connector lines drawn on the canvas. Connected widgets are automatically injected as context for the Copilot session. Connections can be added or removed by dragging from a widget's connector port to the terminal.

{/* Connected widgets on the left */}
📝 Fix the login redirect bug
📄 Architecture notes
🖼️ error-screenshot.png
🖥️ Login prototype
{/* Terminal in the center */}
} > Implement the fix described in the sticky note,`} />

Server-side: each terminal stores its connections in{' '} .storyboard/terminal-{''}.connectedWidgets.json, similar to selectedWidgets.json. Widget content is serialized and passed to Copilot as context on every prompt.

) } /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ Scene 5 — Full flow: connected widgets ↔ copilot ═══════════════════════════════════════════════ */ export function FullFlow() { return (
Scene 5 — Bidirectional Flow

Copilot reads from connected widgets and writes back to them. Changes requested in the terminal are automatically applied to the connected widgets — a sticky note gets updated text, a markdown block gets new content, new widgets get created and auto-connected.

{/* Connected widgets — showing before/after state */}
📝 Fix the login redirect bug
📝 ✓ Login redirect fixed
📄 Architecture notes
📄 + Diff: redirect.js (new)
{/* Terminal showing the completed operation */}
} >
↕ connected widgets are both input and output

The terminal acts as a command center. Connected widgets flow in as context and get mutated by Copilot's actions. New widgets (diffs, summaries) are created on the canvas and auto-connected back to the terminal session.

) }
sources.src/prototypes/main.folder/Example/Forms:
import { Text, FormControl, Button, Stack } from '@primer/react'
import Application from '@/templates/Application/Application.jsx'
import StoryboardForm from '@/components/StoryboardForm/StoryboardForm.jsx'
import TextInput from '@/components/TextInput/TextInput.jsx'
import Checkbox from '@/components/Checkbox/Checkbox.jsx'
import Select from '@/components/Select/Select.jsx'
import Textarea from '@/components/Textarea/Textarea.jsx'
import { useOverride } from '@dfosco/storyboard'
import {
  HomeIcon,
  GearIcon,
} from '@primer/octicons-react'

const topnav = [
  { icon: HomeIcon, label: 'Home', url: '/' },
  { icon: GearIcon, label: 'Forms', url: '/Forms', current: true },
]

function Forms() {
  const [, , clearAll] = useOverride('checkout')

  return (
    <Application title="Storyboard" subtitle="Forms" topnav={topnav}>
      <Text as="h1" fontSize="larger">Form Components Demo</Text>
      <Text as="p" color="fg.muted">
        Type in the fields below, then click Submit to persist values in the URL hash.
        Refresh the page or share the URL to restore state.
      </Text>

      <StoryboardForm data="checkout">
        <Stack direction="vertical" gap="normal" padding="normal">
          <FormControl>
            <FormControl.Label>Email</FormControl.Label>
            <TextInput name="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
          </FormControl>

          <FormControl>
            <FormControl.Label>Full Name</FormControl.Label>
            <TextInput name="name" placeholder="Jane Doe" />
          </FormControl>

          <FormControl>
            <FormControl.Label>Shipping Address</FormControl.Label>
            <Textarea name="address" placeholder="123 Main St..." />
          </FormControl>

          <FormControl>
            <FormControl.Label>Country</FormControl.Label>
            <Select name="country">
              <Select.Option value="">Select a country</Select.Option>
              <Select.Option value="us">United States</Select.Option>
              <Select.Option value="ca">Canada</Select.Option>
              <Select.Option value="uk">United Kingdom</Select.Option>
              <Select.Option value="de">Germany</Select.Option>
            </Select>
          </FormControl>

          <FormControl>
            <Checkbox name="newsletter" />
            <FormControl.Label>Subscribe to newsletter</FormControl.Label>
          </FormControl>

          <Stack direction="horizontal" gap="condensed">
            <Button type="submit" variant="primary">Submit</Button>
            <Button type="button" variant="danger" onClick={() => clearAll()}>
              Reset
            </Button>
          </Stack>
        </Stack>
      </StoryboardForm>
    </Application>
  )
}

export default Forms
